task: I need to leave a handoff note about a room issue for the next shift. It should include the important details clearly so nothing gets missed when they read it later.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a clear handoff note format you can use:

**Handoff Note:**  
Room [number]: [brief issue].  
Please check [specific area/problem] and follow up if needed.  
Guest was informed / not informed.  
Maintenance has been notified / still needs to be notified.  
No further issues noted / additional detail here.

Example:  
**Handoff Note:**  
Room 214: Bathroom sink is draining slowly. Please recheck during next shift and notify maintenance if it’s still backed up. Guest has not been informed yet. No other issues noted.

If you want, I can also make it into a shorter, more front-desk-style note.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s the right idea, but I need it a little more specific. Give me a plain handoff note I can actually use for a room problem — like leak, odor, broken TV, whatever — and keep it short, work-style, no extra wording. I don’t need a template, I need the note.

turns.agent_response: Room [number]: [issue]. Guest [not informed / informed]. Maintenance [notified / not yet notified]. Please follow up on next shift.

user_simulator.text: Close, but that’s still too generic. I need one actual note I can drop in, not brackets and choices. Make it sound like a real housekeeping handoff, short and plain.

turns.agent_response: Room 214: Bathroom sink is leaking under the cabinet. Guest not informed yet. Maintenance not notified yet. Please follow up on next shift.

user_simulator.text: Room 214: Bathroom sink leaking under the cabinet. Guest not informed yet. Maintenance not notified yet. Please follow up next shift.
